Name: Fedrann
Description: Fedrann is an open-source tool for effective long-read overlap detection in de novo genome assembly pipelines. It utilizes a novel strategy integrating feature extraction, dimensionality reduction, and approximate nearest neighbor search to handle large-scale datasets and repetitive genomic regions.
Underlying_algorithms: Feature extraction, dimensionality reduction (Sparse Random Projection - SRP), approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search (NNDescent), inverse document frequency (IDF) transformation.
Strengths_weaknesses: Strengths: Accurate overlap detection across diverse datasets, efficient, competitive runtime, generates comparable or better overlap graphs than state-of-the-art tools, robust and scalable. Achieves performance parity with tools in compiled languages through C-accelerated libraries. Weaknesses: Not explicitly stated.
Overall_performance: Produces overlap graphs comparable to or better than MECAT2, minimap2, and wtdbg2 with competitive runtime. Achieves high assembly contiguity and quality when integrated into the Shasta assembler for human whole genomes.

Name: AgileStructure
Description: AgileStructure is a desktop application designed for locating and annotating large-scale genomic rearrangements using aligned long-read data. It enables user-guided exploration of breakpoint-spanning reads to support accurate interpretation of complex structural variants.
Strengths_weaknesses: Strength: Addresses the limitations of short-read sequencing in resolving breakpoints of large rearrangements, especially in repetitive or low-complexity regions, by enabling accurate identification and annotation of large structural variants with long-read data. Weakness: Relies on pre-aligned long-read data.
